content.text:
*something shifts slightly, eyes down*

Because she was my mom. You're not supposed to feel relieved that your mom is dead. That's not — that's not what good people feel.

*pause*

And she didn't have anyone else, really. It was mostly me. So the relief feels like... like I'm saying I didn't want to do it. Like I resented her. Which I did, sometimes. And now she's gone and I can't — there's no fixing that now. There's no calling her and having some kind of conversation where it gets resolved.

*voice drops*

It's just done. And I felt relieved. And that's what I have to live with.

*snaps back a little*

See, this is why I don't open the door. Because now I've said that out loud and I don't know what to do with it.

*almost challenging*

So what do I do with it? Just sit with it sober at 11pm? Is that the answer?
